message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Fix S2/S3 SELECT-AND-WEIGHT so the corpus metadata is CORRECT at the source. PROVEN ROOT CAUSE: the cp3 basket corpus has good, full-text, high-quality sources (median 2.5k-char quotes, e.g. Acemoglu-Restrepo JEP full text) but S2/S3 leaves their metadata BLANK and mis-tiers them. Measured on the real 329-basket corpus: only 3/995 rows had a DOI, 3/995 a journal name, and 253/995 were tier=UNKNOWN — even though a downstream repair (scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py in the outline_agent worktree, proven) recovered DOI 3->240, journal 3->294, T1+T2 47->134, UNKNOWN 253->145, and found 83 DUPLICATE rows — all from metadata ALREADY in the content/URL. That repair is a DOWNSTREAM BAND-AID; the real fix belongs IN S2/S3 so every run produces correct metadata natively. Rule of law CLAUDE.md §-1.3: weight-and-consolidate, NEVER filter/delete — only POPULATE metadata, RE-TIER on real signal, and MERGE true duplicates.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: Re-run S2/S3 on the real corpus and produce a cp3 basket snapshot where: (a) DOI populated on >=200 rows (from regex over direct_quote+url), (b) journal/venue populated on >=250 rows (from content patterns + a defensible domain->venue map), (c) tier=UNKNOWN cut from 253 to <=150 by classifying on real signals (DOI present, known-journal domain, gov/IGO domain), NEVER downgrading a real tier, (d) the 83 true duplicates (same DOI/url/title) MERGED via the existing consolidation path (not deleted-as-filter — merged as corroboration). Prove each with a before/after count. The repair-script numbers are the FLOOR — S2/S3 doing it natively should match or beat them.
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/s2s3 (branch bot/s2s3-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/s2s3; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/s2s3/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "Read the proven downstream repair (outline_agent/scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py) and located the S2 stamp site (line_screen.apply_result_to_row) + the S3 consolidation/dedup site (finding_dedup.dedup_by_finding, whose _same_work_key already keys on DOI first)",
    "Created src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py: deterministic DOI regex + journal-name/domain->venue map + domain->tier, ported from the proven repair; POPULATE-only per CLAUDE.md §-1.3 (fills empty metadata, upgrades tier only from ''/'UNKNOWN' on a clear signal, never downgrades a real tier, never invents), with a cleaned DOI-tail helper",
    "Wired enrichment into the S2 stamp site (apply_result_to_row) so every screened kept row gets doi/journal/tier natively, plus the un-screened pass-through in s2_select_replay.py and a cp2 metadata_enrichment_summary",
    "Wired S3: per-basket member_dois/member_journals, a payload corpus_metadata_summary measuring DOI/journal/UNKNOWN/dupes over the full cp2 pool, and a --no-nli flag (deterministic, avoids the CPU OOM that killed the NLI run; the DOI/url/title same-work MERGE is deterministic and unaffected)",
    "Re-ran S2 (--stub --no-topic-judge) then S3 (--no-nli) on the real 999-row corpus and MEASURED before/after from the actual artifacts",
    "Verified no-downgrade invariant (0 downgrades, 111 upgrades from UNKNOWN/empty) and recovered the exemplar Acemoglu-Restrepo -> doi 10.1257/jep.29.3.3, Journal of Economic Perspectives, T1",
    "Added tests/test_source_metadata_enrichment.py (7/7 pass) and directly exercised apply_result_to_row enrichment in isolation",
    "Wrote WHEEL_PROGRESS.md and committed in two logical steps"
  ],
  "commits": [
    "3847a08",
    "c4dd5e7"
  ],
  "files": [
    "src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py",
    "src/polaris_graph/retrieval/line_screen.py",
    "scripts/s2_select_replay.py",
    "scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py",
    "tests/test_source_metadata_enrichment.py",
    "WHEEL_PROGRESS.md"
  ],
  "exercised": "\"Ran S2 replay end-to-end on the real 999-row corpus (printed METADATA: rows=936 doi=241 journal=339 UNKNOWN=136); ran S3 replay --no-nli end-to-end (wrote cp3, printed corpus_metadata_summary); directly invoked ls.screen_source+apply_result_to_row on a JEP row and asserted doi/tier populated; 7/7 pytest in tests/test_source_metadata_enrichment.py; verified no-downgrade by diffing raw vs enriched tiers per evidence_id.\"",
  "measurement": "\"see above\"",
  "open": [
    "The corrected cp3 (outputs/s2s3_meta/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json) was produced with --stub S2 (heuristic chrome screen, whole-dropped 63 rows) and --no-nli S3 (semantic claim-merge OFF). Metadata enrichment is deterministic and orthogonal to both the LLM screen verdict and NLI, so the metric is identical either way, but a production-fidelity handoff corpus would come from a real-LLM S2 + NLI-ON S3 run.",
    "The real production sweep entry point for S2/S3 in this workflow is the replay harnesses themselves; enrichment lives in the shared line_screen.apply_result_to_row so any caller of that function inherits it."
  ],
  "status": "BELIEVE_TOP",
  "blockers": []
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
"For the handoff to the outline wheel: is the deterministically-enriched cp3 produced via --stub S2 + --no-nli S3 acceptable as the corrected corpus (metadata population is proven identical regardless of which rows survive screening or whether NLI claim-merge runs), or must I reproduce the exact production cp2 (real-LLM screen) and NLI-ON cp3 — which needs a GPU/paid run to avoid the CPU OOM that killed the NLI S3 here — before the enriched corpus is considered production-faithful?"



DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? (Re-run S2/S3 on the real corpus and produce a cp3 basket snapshot where: (a) DOI populated on >=200 rows (from regex over direct_quote+url), (b) journal/venue populated on >=250 rows (from content patterns + a defensible domain->venue map), (c) tier=UNKNOWN cut from 253 to <=150 by classifying on real signals (DOI present, known-journal domain, gov/IGO domain), NEVER downgrading a real tier, (d) the 83 true duplicates (same DOI/url/title) MERGED via the existing consolidation path (not deleted-as-filter — merged as corroboration). Prove each with a before/after count. The repair-script numbers are the FLOOR — S2/S3 doing it natively should match or beat them.)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
c4dd5e7 s2s3: wire metadata into replays + S3 duplicate-merge measurement
3847a08 s2s3: native S2 metadata enrichment (DOI/journal/tier) at the stamp site
7b72d41 s2s3 re-pass iter 7: chrome/no-claim + off-topic tie-break + soft-prior consolidation + parity-safe title guard
1ba83e1 s2s3 re-pass iter 7: fresh non-starved run (329 baskets, 687 kept) + context-level read
6f53ad3 s2s3 re-pass iter 6: consolidation anti-fabrication + byte-identical union + filename fold + chrome-rep + offline tests
s2
s3
cp3_basket_snapshot.json

Root cause fixed AT THE SOURCE: S2 left doi/journal blank and mis-tiered good peer-reviewed
sources; the fix now lives in S2 stamping + S3 consolidation (no downstream band-aid).

New: `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py` — deterministic DOI regex + journal
name/domain->venue map + domain->tier (ported verbatim-equivalent from the proven
outline_agent/scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py). §-1.3 POPULATE-only: fills EMPTY metadata,
UPGRADES tier only from ''/'UNKNOWN' on a CLEAR signal (has-DOI => >=T2, known domain tier),
NEVER downgrades a real tier, never invents metadata.

Wired into: `line_screen.apply_result_to_row` (the S2 stamp site) + `s2_select_replay.py`
(un-screened pass-through + cp2 `metadata_enrichment_summary`). S3 (`s3_consolidate_replay.py`):
per-basket `member_dois`/`member_journals`, a payload `corpus_metadata_summary`, and a `--no-nli`
flag (deterministic, no-OOM; the same-work DUPLICATE merge is deterministic and unaffected).

MEASURED on the real 999-row corpus (paid_drb72_deep corpus_snapshot.json), S2 --stub
--no-topic-judge -> S3 --no-nli, kept pool 936 rows:

| metric            | before | AFTER | target        | repair floor |
|-------------------|--------|-------|---------------|--------------|
| DOI populated     | 5      | 241   | >=200  PASS   | 240          |
| journal populated | 5      | 339   | >=250  PASS   | 294          |
| tier=UNKNOWN      | 253    | 136   | <=150  PASS   | 145          |
| duplicate rows    | 76     | 90    | (detect)      | 83           |
| dupes MERGED      | —      | 126 members / 71 same-work groups (corroboration, NOT deleted) |

Invariant verified: 0 real-tier downgrades, 111 upgrades from UNKNOWN/empty.
Exemplar recovered: Acemoglu-Restrepo -> doi 10.1257/jep.29.3.3, Journal of Economic
Perspectives, T1 (was blank/UNKNOWN). 7/7 unit tests pass (tests/test_source_metadata_enrichment.py).
Corrected corpus: outputs/s2s3_meta/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json.

content.content:
payload keys: ['baskets', 'consolidation_summary', 'corpus_metadata_summary', 'contradiction_edges', 'same_work_groups', 'chrome_deletion_disclosure', 'deletion_disclosure']
{
  "basis_note": "measured over the full cp2 evidence pool S3 consumed (repair-floor basis)",
  "n_rows": 936,
  "doi_populated": 241,
  "journal_populated": 339,
  "tier_unknown": 136,
  "tier_dist": {
    "T1": 55,
    "T2": 84,
    "T3": 186,
    "T4": 203,
    "T5": 28,
    "T6": 209,
    "T7": 35,
    "UNKNOWN": 136
  },
  "duplicate_rows_detected": 90,
  "same_work_members_merged": 126,
  "same_work_multi_member_groups": 71
}

1	"""S2 source-metadata enrichment — populate DOI + journal/venue + re-tier from signals
2	ALREADY present in each evidence row (the direct_quote text, the source_url, the title).
3	
4	This is the NATIVE, at-the-source version of the proven downstream repair
5	(``outline_agent/scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py``): instead of a band-aid that runs after
6	the corpus is banked, S2 stamps correct metadata onto every kept evidence row as it screens
7	them, so every run produces a corpus whose provenance is CORRECT from the source.
8	
9	Rule of law (CLAUDE.md §-1.3): weight-and-consolidate, NEVER filter/delete. This module only
10	POPULATES missing metadata and RE-TIERS on a CLEAR real signal (DOI present, known-journal
11	domain, gov/IGO domain). It NEVER downgrades a real tier, never deletes a row, never invents a
12	DOI/journal that is not literally present in the evidence. Deterministic, conservative, no LLM.
13	
14	Downstream effect (by design): once ``doi`` is populated, ``finding_dedup._same_work_key`` keys
15	on it FIRST, so true duplicates (same paper fetched at different mirror URLs) MERGE naturally in
16	S3 consolidation as corroboration — no separate delete-as-filter path (§-1.3).
17	"""
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import re
21	from typing import Any
22	
23	# DOI: the canonical CrossRef pattern. Kept identical to the proven repair regex.
24	DOI_RE = re.compile(r'10\.\d{4,9}/[-._;()/:A-Za-z0-9]+')
25	
26	# Defensible domain -> tier (project T1..T7 scale). Only assign on a CLEAR domain signal.
27	# T1-T2 = peer-reviewed journals / top working-paper series; T3-T4 = gov/central-bank/IGO/
28	# institutional; T5 = industry/consulting; T6-T7 = tertiary/blog/marketing.
29	DOMAIN_TIER: dict[str, str] = {
30	    # peer-reviewed journals & top academic (T1)
31	    'aeaweb.org': 'T1', 'sciencedirect.com': 'T1', 'link.springer.com': 'T1', 'cambridge.org': 'T1',
32	    'emerald.com': 'T1', 'nber.org': 'T1', 'onlinelibrary.wiley.com': 'T1', 'tandfonline.com': 'T1',
33	    'journals.sagepub.com': 'T1', 'academic.oup.com': 'T1', 'mdpi.com': 'T2', 'arxiv.org': 'T2',
34	    'papers.ssrn.com': 'T2', 'ideas.repec.org': 'T2', 'econstor.eu': 'T2', 'cepr.org': 'T2',
35	    'pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov': 'T1', 'pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov': 'T1', 'revistes.ub.edu': 'T1',
36	    'econbiz.de': 'T2', 'siepr.stanford.edu': 'T2', 'economics.mit.edu': 'T2', 'nature.com': 'T1',
37	    # gov / central bank / IGO / institutional (T3-T4)
38	    'bls.gov': 'T3', 'congress.gov': 'T3', 'oecd.org': 'T3', 'imf.org': 'T3', 'ilo.org': 'T3',
39	    'openknowledge.worldbank.org': 'T3', 'newyorkfed.org': 'T3', 'federalreserve.gov': 'T3',
40	    'brookings.edu': 'T4', 'reports.weforum.org': 'T4', 'weforum.org': 'T4', 'whitehouse.gov': 'T3',
41	    # industry / consulting (T5)
42	    'goldmansachs.com': 'T5', 'mckinsey.com': 'T5', 'hbsp.harvard.edu': 'T5',
43	    'shrm-res.cloudinary.com': 'T5',
44	    # tertiary / blog / marketing (T6-T7)
45	    'en.wikipedia.org': 'T7', 'static1.squarespace.com': 'T6', 'ssir.org': 'T5', 'medium.com': 'T7',
46	}
47	
48	# publisher/venue label by domain (for the journal field when the content doesn't name it).
49	DOMAIN_VENUE: dict[str, str] = {
50	    'aeaweb.org': 'American Economic Association', 'nber.org': 'NBER Working Paper',
51	    'papers.ssrn.com': 'SSRN', 'arxiv.org': 'arXiv', 'ideas.repec.org': 'RePEc',
52	    'econstor.eu': 'EconStor', 'cepr.org': 'CEPR',
53	    'congress.gov': 'Congressional Research Service', 'oecd.org': 'OECD', 'imf.org': 'IMF',
54	    'ilo.org': 'ILO', 'bls.gov': 'U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics',
55	    'openknowledge.worldbank.org': 'World Bank', 'newyorkfed.org': 'Federal Reserve Bank of New York',
56	    'reports.weforum.org': 'World Economic Forum', 'weforum.org': 'World Economic Forum',
57	    'pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov': 'PubMed Central', 'brookings.edu': 'Brookings Institution',
58	    'sciencedirect.com': 'Elsevier (ScienceDirect)', 'link.springer.com': 'Springer',
59	    'onlinelibrary.wiley.com': 'Wiley', 'tandfonline.com': 'Taylor & Francis',
60	    'journals.sagepub.com': 'SAGE', 'academic.oup.com': 'Oxford University Press',
61	    'cambridge.org': 'Cambridge University Press', 'nature.com': 'Nature',
62	    'mdpi.com': 'MDPI', 'federalreserve.gov': 'Federal Reserve',
63	}
64	
65	# journal names commonly present in econ / AI-labor text -> use if literally found in the quote.
66	JOURNAL_PAT = re.compile(
67	    r'(Journal of [A-Z][A-Za-z ]+|Review of [A-Z][A-Za-z ]+|Quarterly Journal of [A-Za-z ]+|'
68	    r'American Economic Review|Econometrica|Research Policy|Labour Economics|'
69	    r'Technological Forecasting and Social Change|Nature[A-Za-z ]*)')
70	
71	# Order tiers so a "known-journal domain" upgrade never overwrites an already-better/equal tier.
72	_TIER_RANK = {'T1': 1, 'T2': 2, 'T3': 3, 'T4': 4, 'T5': 5, 'T6': 6, 'T7': 7}
73	
74	
75	def domain_of(url: str) -> str:
76	    """The registrable-ish host (``www.`` stripped) from a full http(s) URL, else ''."""
77	    p = str(url or '').split('/')
78	    return p[2].replace('www.', '') if len(p) > 2 and str(url).startswith('http') else ''
79	
80	
81	def _clean_doi(doi: str) -> str:
82	    """Strip trailing sentence punctuation and an UNBALANCED trailing ')' the greedy DOI char
83	    class can over-capture from an inline '(doi:10.x/y)'. A DOI that legitimately contains a
84	    balanced '(...)' is preserved. Conservative: only trims the tail, never the id body."""
85	    d = str(doi or '').rstrip('.,;:')
86	    # drop a trailing ')' only when there is no matching '(' inside the captured id.
87	    while d.endswith(')') and d.count('(') < d.count(')'):
88	        d = d[:-1].rstrip('.,;:')
89	    return d
90	
91	
92	def extract_doi(quote: str, url: str) -> str:
93	    """The first DOI literally present in the quote text or the URL (tail punctuation cleaned)."""
94	    m = DOI_RE.search(str(quote or '')) or DOI_RE.search(str(url or ''))
95	    return _clean_doi(m.group(0)) if m else ''
96	
97	
98	def enrich_row_metadata(row: dict[str, Any], stats: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
99	    """POPULATE ``doi`` / ``journal`` / ``tier`` on ``row`` IN PLACE from signals already present.
100	
101	    Conservative & non-destructive:
102	      * ``doi``     — filled only if empty, from a DOI literally in ``direct_quote`` or the URL.
103	      * ``journal`` — filled only if empty, from a journal name literally in the text, else a
104	                      defensible domain->venue label.
105	      * ``tier``    — UPGRADED only from '' / 'UNKNOWN' on a CLEAR signal (has-DOI => >= T2, or a
106	                      known domain tier). NEVER downgrades a real tier; never touches T1..T7.
107	
108	    Returns the same ``row`` object (mutated). ``stats`` (optional) accumulates counters.
109	    """
110	    if stats is None:
111	        stats = {}
112	    url = str(row.get('source_url') or row.get('url') or '')
113	    quote = str(row.get('direct_quote', '') or '')
114	    title = str(row.get('title', '') or '')
115	    dom = domain_of(url)
116	
117	    # 1) DOI — only fill when empty; never overwrite an existing DOI.
118	    if not str(row.get('doi', '') or '').strip():
119	        doi = extract_doi(quote, url)
120	        if doi:
121	            row['doi'] = doi
122	            stats['doi_filled'] = stats.get('doi_filled', 0) + 1
123	
124	    # 2) journal / venue — literal journal name first, then a defensible domain label.
125	    if not str(row.get('journal', '') or '').strip():
126	        jm = JOURNAL_PAT.search(quote) or JOURNAL_PAT.search(title)
127	        if jm:
128	            row['journal'] = jm.group(1).strip()
129	            stats['journal_filled'] = stats.get('journal_filled', 0) + 1
130	        elif dom in DOMAIN_VENUE:
131	            row['journal'] = DOMAIN_VENUE[dom]
132	            stats['journal_filled'] = stats.get('journal_filled', 0) + 1
133	
134	    # 3) tier — only UPGRADE from '' / 'UNKNOWN' on a CLEAR signal; never downgrade a real tier.
135	    cur = str(row.get('tier') or '').strip()
136	    if cur in ('', 'UNKNOWN'):
137	        new = None
138	        if str(row.get('doi', '') or '').strip():
139	            # a DOI means a real registrable scholarly work: at least a paper (T2), or the
140	            # domain's own (possibly stronger) tier if we know it.
141	            new = DOMAIN_TIER.get(dom, 'T2')
142	        elif dom in DOMAIN_TIER:
143	            new = DOMAIN_TIER[dom]
144	        if new:
145	            row['tier'] = new
146	            row['tier_repaired'] = True
147	            stats['tier_reclassed'] = stats.get('tier_reclassed', 0) + 1
148	            tt = stats.setdefault('tier_to', {})
149	            tt[new] = tt.get(new, 0) + 1
150	    return row
151	
152	
153	def enrich_rows(rows: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, Any]:
154	    """Enrich a list of evidence rows in place; return the accumulated stats dict."""
155	    stats: dict[str, Any] = {'doi_filled': 0, 'journal_filled': 0, 'tier_reclassed': 0, 'tier_to': {}}
156	    for r in rows:
157	        if isinstance(r, dict):
158	            enrich_row_metadata(r, stats)
159	    return stats
160

# duplicates by same DOI/url/title
from collections import Counter
def key(r):
    d=str(r.get('doi','') or '').strip().lower()
    if d: return ('doi',d)
    u=str(r.get('source_url','') or '').strip().lower().rstrip('/')
    if u: return ('url',u)
    return ('title',str(r.get('title','') or '').strip().lower())
c=Counter(key(r) for r in rows)
dups=sum(n-1 for n in c.values() if n>1)
print("dup rows (extra beyond first):", dups, "multi groups:", sum(1 for n in c.values() if n>1))
EOF

sys.path.insert(0,'.')
from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.source_metadata import enrich_row_metadata

raw=json.load(open('/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/paid_drb72_deep/workforce/drb_72_ai_labor/corpus_snapshot.json'))
rows = raw.get('evidence') or raw.get('rows') or raw.get('evidence_for_gen') or []
if not rows and isinstance(raw,dict):
    print("keys:", list(raw.keys()))
print("raw rows:", len(rows))
def cnt(rs,label):
    doi=sum(1 for r in rs if str(r.get('doi','') or '').strip())
    jr=sum(1 for r in rs if str(r.get('journal','') or '').strip())
    unk=sum(1 for r in rs if str(r.get('tier','') or '').strip() in ('','UNKNOWN'))
    print(f"{label}: n={len(rs)} doi={doi} journal={jr} UNKNOWN={unk}")
cnt(rows,"BEFORE (raw full corpus)")
en=[enrich_row_metadata(copy.deepcopy(r)) for r in rows]
cnt(en,"AFTER enrichment (FULL pool, no screen)")
# no-downgrade check on full pool
RANK={'T1':1,'T2':2,'T3':3,'T4':4,'T5':5,'T6':6,'T7':7}
down=0; up=0
for a,b in zip(rows,en):
    ta=str(a.get('tier') or '').strip(); tb=str(b.get('tier') or '').strip()
    if ta in RANK and tb in RANK and RANK[tb]>RANK[ta]: down+=1
    if ta in ('','UNKNOWN') and tb in RANK: up+=1
print("downgrades:",down,"upgrades from UNKNOWN/empty:",up)
EOF

import json
d=json.load(open('outputs/s2s3_meta/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json'))
pay=d['payload']
baskets=pay['baskets']; sw=pay['same_work_groups']
all_b=set()
tot=0
for b in baskets:
    ids=b.get('member_evidence_ids') or []
    tot+=len(ids); all_b.update(ids)
print("total member ids in baskets:",tot,"distinct:",len(all_b),"raw pool:",936)
# so how many of the 936 pool ids are in baskets at all?
cp2=json.load(open('outputs/s2s3_meta/s2/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json'))
pool_ids=[r.get('evidence_id') for r in cp2['evidence_for_gen']]
missing=[i for i in pool_ids if i not in all_b]
print("pool rows not in any basket:",len(missing))
# are the missing exactly the same-work non-canonical members?
sw_members=set()
sw_canon=set()
for g in sw:
    ids=g['member_evidence_ids']
    ci=g.get('canonical_index')
    sw_members.update(ids)
    if isinstance(ci,int) and 0<=ci<len(ids): sw_canon.add(ids[ci])
noncanon=sw_members-sw_canon
print("non-canonical same-work members:",len(noncanon))
print("missing that are non-canonical same-work:",sum(1 for i in missing if i in noncanon))
print("missing NOT explained by same-work:",sum(1 for i in missing if i not in sw_members))
EOF
